category_title: Minimally verbal young children with severe ASD and language processing deficits
minimal_context: The student is in a structured intervention setting working one-on-one with a therapist to match common objects to an array of exact photos.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Here is a cup. Match the object to the picture.

STUDENT: (Does not respond, requiring prompting to listen to the verbal stimuli and pick the correct picture from the array.)

TUTOR: Listen to me. Pick the picture of the cup.

STUDENT: (Remains silent, not producing any meaningful vocalizations or words during the assessment.)

TUTOR: Try again. Look at the photos and match the cup.

STUDENT: (Performs inconsistently in object and picture identification, picking the wrong photo from the array of three pictures, reflecting her ability to identify common objects and pictures correctly only 10% of the time.)

options.A: Prompt the student to match common objects, such as a cup, spoon, comb, and key, to the exact photos first.
options.B: Turn toward the student and issue a statement of concern, such as 'What do you need?', along with brief physical contact.
options.C: Use a 'request for clarification,' asking the student for explanations and elaborations regarding the matching task.
options.D: Immediately remove all demands and task materials, such as the objects and photos, for 60 seconds.
